evals.prompt: Review the paper at build/main.pdf in constructive mode. This is a paper about efficient video object tracking using tile-level sampling and polyomino-based spatial pruning.
evals.expected_output: A structured VLDB review in constructive mode with summary, strengths, weaknesses, detailed comments, questions, and assessment summary table.
expectations: Review follows VLDB structure with all required sections (Summary, Strengths, Weaknesses, Detailed Comments, Questions, Assessment Summary)
Review correctly identifies the paper's core contribution (tile-level spatial sampling with polyominoes for video ETL/object tracking)
Review references specific related work from the video DBMS literature (e.g., OTIF, LEAP, NoScope, EVA)
Detailed comments reference specific sections, figures, or equations from the paper
Weaknesses include actionable suggestions for improvement (not just complaints)
Review identifies the stationary-camera assumption as a limitation
Review includes an overall rating from the VLDB scale

evals.prompt: Review the paper at build/main.pdf in adversarial mode. This is a paper about efficient video object tracking using tile-level sampling and polyomino-based spatial pruning.
evals.expected_output: A structured VLDB review in adversarial mode with harder scrutiny on novelty, evaluation gaps, and generality claims.
expectations: Review follows VLDB structure with all required sections
Review scrutinizes novelty claims — questions what is genuinely new vs. engineering combination of known techniques
Review pushes on evaluation gaps (e.g., limited baselines, dataset diversity, parameter sensitivity)
Review questions generality of the approach beyond stationary cameras
Review compares explicitly against the strongest related work (OTIF, LEAP) and questions whether comparisons are fair
Tone is tough but fair — acknowledges real contributions even while being critical
Review rating is lower or equal to the constructive mode rating

evals.prompt: Review the paper at apperception_optimization.pdf in constructive mode. This is a paper about a geospatial video analytics system with spatial-aware optimizations.
evals.expected_output: A structured VLDB review in constructive mode. We can compare this against real reviews the paper received.
expectations: Review follows VLDB structure with all required sections
Review correctly identifies the build-filter-observe paradigm and S-Flow DSL as core contributions
Review identifies issues similar to those raised by the real VLDB reviewers (e.g., novelty concerns, data model formalization, evaluation comprehensiveness)
Review references relevant related work (e.g., OTIF, EVA, VisualWorldDB, Apperception demo, Visual Road)
Detailed comments are specific enough that authors would know exactly what to fix
Review includes an overall rating consistent with the mixed reception the paper actually received (Weak Reject to Weak Accept range)
